rules.rule: When a small, all-black cowbird shows a conspicuous bright red or orange-red iris that is clearly visible as a bold colored eye, combined with a distinctly thick-based, slightly decurved bill that appears heavier and more robust than a typical icterid bill, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.
preconditions: Bird is entirely or nearly entirely black-plumaged (male)
Iris is visibly bright red or orange-red — not dark brown or black
Bill appears notably thick-based and slightly decurved (gonys curved downward), giving a heavier, almost grosbeak-like profile compared to Shiny Cowbird's slimmer, straighter bill
Body posture is compact and relatively heavy-bodied
No strong iridescent blue-green gloss is the dominant plumage impression (Bronzed shows bronzy/purple gloss, Shiny shows blue-green)

Bird shows a visible ruff or hackle-like feathering on the neck/nape — an erectile ruff that males can flare during display, which is a distinctive structural feature of Bronzed Cowbird absent in Shiny Cowbird
Head appears proportionally large and rounded, with a high-crowned, almost bull-necked profile characteristic of Bronzed Cowbird
Bill length is moderate to short relative to head size — not elongated or spike-like as would be atypical for this species
No streaking, spotting, or brown tones visible on the plumage (ruling out females or immatures of either species)

rules.rule: When a black cowbird on the ground shows a visibly thick, heavy, conical bill with a distinctly rounded head profile (approaching a 'bull-necked' or large-headed appearance), and the plumage shows a dull-to-moderate gloss rather than intense iridescent sheen across the entire body, identify as Bronzed Cowbird.
preconditions: Bird is entirely or predominantly black/dark-plumaged
Bill appears noticeably thick, deep-based, and heavy relative to head size — distinctly more robust than a typical icterid
Head appears large, rounded, and somewhat 'bull-necked' or hunched in posture

No strongly iridescent blue-green sheen visible across the entire body (Shiny Cowbird shows intense gloss); plumage may show some bronze/greenish gloss but appears more matte or dully glossed in typical light
Bird is cowbird-sized (smaller than a grackle, larger than a sparrow)
